originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&pid_output` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= MARLIN_H true) (= PIDTEMP false) (= PID_DEBUG false))
model:
(define-fun PID_DEBUG () Bool
  false)
(define-fun MARLIN_H () Bool
  true)
(define-fun PIDTEMP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: When PIDTEMP=F, the entire #ifdef PIDTEMP block (L13-20) takes the #else branch: pid_output=1 at L11, then pid_output=0 at L19 (overwriting L11), but pid_output is never read. Dead store. The PID_DEBUG and SERIAL_ECHO_START code is inside #ifdef PIDTEMP, so it is never reached when PIDTEMP=F — MARLIN_H and PID_DEBUG are irrelevant in this path. No '// ERROR' for pid_output dead store → new bug not in VBDB. The dead store occurs for any config with PIDTEMP=F. PC adds MARLIN_H=T and PID_DEBUG=F unnecessarily. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
